patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-05 (MENU-ENABLEMENT-DOC-RECONCILE — registry-driven DYNAMIC menu enablement is COMPLETE: the menu now re-resolves PER FRAME against a live `PredicateContext` and greys items via the canonical registry path; the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff` / `play_item_enabled` greying is RETIRED; supersedes the W08-ACCELERATOR snapshot's "registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve … deferred" line below): #313–#314 land it in two carves. **#313 (`459c689`, MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE)** added the editor-ui "disabled-but-visible" substrate — a separate `MenuEntry.enabled` predicate (distinct from the visibility `predicate`, which FILTERS the entry out), `ResolvedEntry.enabled` computed in `resolve` WITHOUT filtering (disabled entries stay present + keep their accelerator), an `enabled_command_for_shortcut` execution resolver (the binding-lookup `command_for_shortcut` is UNCHANGED, so the W08.3 parity guard holds), and `PredicateContext` `can_play`/`can_pause`/`can_stop`/`can_step` + `is_editing` booleans; `default_editor_menu` gates File Open/Save/Save-As on `is_editing` and each Play item on its `can_*` (additive + behaviour-neutral — no consumer read `enabled` yet). **#314 (`10790ba`, MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE)** wired the consumers: `editor-egui-host` caches the `MenuRegistry` and RE-RESOLVES it each frame against the live `PredicateContext` (`project_main_menu` → `(label, accel, command, enabled)` 4-tuples), greying each item via `ResolvedEntry.enabled` → `add_enabled`; `editor-shell` publishes a live `PredicateContext` each frame (`predicate_context()` — `can_*`/`is_editing` from `PlayState`, `has_selection` from the entity selection) through a new `Handoff<PredicateContext>` (aliased host-side, so editor-state gains no editor-ui edge) and routes the keyboard `window_event` path via `enabled_command_for_shortcut` (so a greyed accelerator — e.g. `Ctrl+S` while a play session is active — does NOT fire); the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff` / `MenuStateSnapshot` (file DELETED) / `play_item_enabled` Play-greying channel is RETIRED — ONE canonical enablement path. **Rough-edge fix:** the File `Save`/`Open`/`Save-As` items now grey out OUTSIDE the Editing state (where they no-op) — previously they looked clickable but silently no-op'd during Play; Play items grey via the SAME registry path. This SUPERSEDES the W08-ACCELERATOR snapshot's "STILL DEFERRED: the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (the menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; the shortcut→command index is static because every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional)" line (entries now carry enablement predicates; the host re-resolves per frame with the live context) AND the PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC (#302) snapshot's bespoke-greying framing (that channel is retired). **STILL DEFERRED:** Play/View accelerator DISPLAY + execution (Play's real keys are the plain `Space`/`Escape` PIE binds, not menu accelerators; View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding); the `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI / conflict population (computed every resolve, still unsurfaced); dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View); plugin menu entries; host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement (menu CLICKS still enqueue through the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO → `route_menu_command`; #314 unified the KEYBOARD's enablement onto the registry path, NOT the click transport); generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution beyond the canonical entries; the VISIBILITY predicates (the filtering `predicate`) remain available but UNUSED by `default_editor_menu` — all entries stay visible, only enablement varies. The W08-ACCELERATOR / #304 / PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-04 (W08-ACCELERATOR-DOC-RECONCILE — the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION thread is COMPLETE: File/Edit keyboard accelerators now ROUTE through the canonical menu, which is the single source of truth; supersedes the #304 snapshot's "display-only / EXECUTION deferred" line below): #308–#311 land File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION. **#308 (`453a569`, W08-CANONICAL-MENU-SOURCE)** moved the default editor menu into `editor-ui` (`menus::default_menu::default_editor_menu` — the canonical File/Edit/Play/View definition) and added `ResolveResult::command_for_shortcut(&Shortcut) -> Option<&Command>` (an O(1), first-registered-wins index built in `resolve()`), so the host menu bar and editor-shell build from ONE definition with no reverse crate edge. **#309 (`9004b4b`, W08-2-KEYCODE-PARITY)** added the shell-local `lifecycle::accelerator::keycode_to_shortcut` (`rge_input::KeyCode` + Ctrl/Shift → `rge_editor_ui::menus::Shortcut`; it lives in editor-shell because `editor-ui ↛ rge-input`, forbidden-dep rule 4) plus a keyboard/menu PARITY guard — with NO live-path change yet. **#310 (`4fc66cf`, W08-3-KEYSTROKE-CUTOVER)** made the cutover live: `editor-shell`'s `window_event` resolves each un-consumed `KeyDown` via `keycode_to_shortcut → command_for_shortcut` and dispatches the bound `Command` through the shared `EditorShell::route_menu_command` sink — collapsing the former `EditorKeyCommand` Save/Save-As/Undo/Redo arm and the inline `Ctrl+O` arm into one menu-sourced path (and making the old Shift-sloppy `Ctrl+Shift+O` a precise no-op). **#311 (`e98f41d`, W08-4-RETIRE-SHADOW)** retired the now-shadow `EditorKeyCommand::{Undo,Redo,Save,SaveAsProject}` variants — the File/Edit keystroke→command literals live ONLY in the canonical menu now — and re-anchored the parity guard to pin "the menu binds these five accelerators AND `from_key_press` returns None for them" (the invariant became "menu is canonical, no shadow", not "two maps agree"). This SUPERSEDES the #304 snapshot's "**Authority:** mirror the live, executing `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm" framing — the menu is now the canonical SOURCE of that routing (those binds are retired) — and realizes its "the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION work will unify the two" forward-pointer. **STILL DEFERRED** (narrowing #304's "accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population" line — only the **File/Edit EXECUTION** half is now shipped): Play/View accelerator display AND execution (Play's real keys are the plain `Space`/`Escape` PIE binds, NOT menu accelerators; View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding); the `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI / conflict population; the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (the menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; the shortcut→command index is static because every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional); dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View); plugin menu entries; host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement (menu CLICKS still enqueue through the `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO → `drain_and_route_menu_commands` → `route_menu_command`; W08 unified the KEYBOARD onto that sink, it did NOT replace the click FIFO); generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution beyond the five File/Edit binds. The time-scale binds (`Ctrl+2/0/4`), `Space`/`Escape` playback, and plain-`R` reload remain documented execution-only keybinds with no menu home (an intentional asymmetry retained on `EditorKeyCommand` / the playback + reload axes). The #304 / PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
 
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-04 (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE — File/Edit menu items now show their keyboard accelerators, and the menu construction is its own submodule; the menu-deepening thread is closed): #304 (`284998e`, MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY) makes the **File + Edit** menu items render their real keyboard accelerator next to the label — `Open…` Ctrl+O, `Save` Ctrl+S, `Save As New Project…` Ctrl+Shift+S, `Undo` Ctrl+Z, `Redo` Ctrl+Y — via egui's right-aligned `shortcut_text`, sourced from the existing `editor-ui::menus::Shortcut` substrate carried on `MenuEntry.shortcut` (the projection widened from `(label, Command)` to `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`; a `menu_item` render helper paints each `egui::Button` + optional `shortcut_text`). **Display-only:** clicking still dispatches through the host→shell FIFO — the accelerator is a passive hint, NO command-execution / keystroke-routing / crate-edge change. **File/Edit only (Play/View deferred, `None`):** the shown values mirror the live, executing editor-shell keystroke routing (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm), pinned by host tests; the host CANNOT import editor-shell (reverse edge), so `MenuEntry.shortcut` (editor-ui) is the designated home and the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION work will unify the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`. Play's only shortcut data is the play-toolbar's ADVISORY `F5/Esc` hints, which don't match the real Space/Escape PIE binds — surfacing them would open the advisory-vs-real accelerator/execution conflict this beat avoids; View ▸ Reset Camera has no binding. **Then #305 (`892a0ad`, EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION) extracted the menu-construction block** (the four extension-point consts + `build_main_menu_entries` + `play_item_enabled` + `menu_item`) VERBATIM out of `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` into a new `menu.rs` submodule — behaviour-identical (render path + every test assertion byte-unchanged) — dropping lib.rs 1062 → 836 lines and **RETIRING the `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation** (0 markers crate-wide; mirrors EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION #301). This narrows the prior snapshot's "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population" deferred line — the **DISPLAY** half shipped, for **File/Edit**. STILL DEFERRED: accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population (Play/View accelerator display deferred with it); the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve; dynamic toggle LABELS; plugin menu entries; generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO); dynamic enablement for the File / Edit / View menus. The PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.

 2026-06-04 (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE — planning-substrate hygiene after the File/Edit accelerator beat + the menu-module extraction, docs-only). +### 2026-06-05 — Registry-driven DYNAMIC menu enablement shipped; bespoke Play-greying retired (#313 + #314)
+
+**Forward-only follow-up (MENU-ENABLEMENT-DOC-RECONCILE).** Narrows the W08-accelerator (#308–#311) subsection's "Still open — the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (the menu resolves once with `PredicateContext::default()`; the shortcut→command index is static because every `default_editor_menu` entry is unconditional)" line, and supersedes the PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC (#302) bespoke-greying mechanism: dynamic menu enablement is now the ONE canonical registry path, re-resolved per frame against a live `PredicateContext`, and the bespoke `MenuStateHandoff` / `play_item_enabled` channel is retired. Records the two merged PRs.
+
+**Now shipped — registry-driven dynamic enablement (#313 + #314).**
+- **#313 (`459c689`, MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE)** — editor-ui "disabled-but-visible" substrate: a separate `MenuEntry.enabled` predicate (distinct from the visibility `predicate`, which FILTERS the entry out); `ResolvedEntry.enabled` computed in `resolve` WITHOUT filtering (disabled entries stay present + keep their accelerator); `enabled_command_for_shortcut` (the keyboard EXECUTION resolver — `command_for_shortcut` keeps its binding/display semantics, so the W08.3 parity guard is intact); `PredicateContext` gains `can_play`/`can_pause`/`can_stop`/`can_step` + `is_editing`. `default_editor_menu` gates File Open/Save/Save-As on `is_editing` and each Play item on its `can_*`. Additive + behaviour-neutral (no consumer read `enabled` yet).
+- **#314 (`10790ba`, MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE)** — wired the consumers + retired the bespoke channel. `editor-egui-host` caches the `MenuRegistry` and RE-RESOLVES it each frame via `project_main_menu(&registry, &ctx)` → `(label, accel, command, enabled)`, greying each item via `add_enabled(enabled)`. `editor-shell` publishes a live `PredicateContext` each frame (`predicate_context()` — `can_*`/`is_editing` from `PlayState`, `has_selection` from the entity selection) through a new `Handoff<PredicateContext>` (aliased host-side, so editor-state gains no editor-ui edge), and routes the keyboard `window_event` path via `enabled_command_for_shortcut`. The bespoke `MenuStateHandoff` / `MenuStateSnapshot` (file deleted) / `play_item_enabled` Play-greying is RETIRED.
+
+**Rough-edge fix.** File `Save`/`Open`/`Save-As` grey out outside the Editing state (where they no-op) — previously always-enabled, so they looked clickable but silently no-op'd during Play. Play items grey via the SAME registry path (no longer a separate mechanism). Keyboard: `Ctrl+S` while greyed no longer fires (was a PIE-gated handler no-op + warn-log; net behaviour identical, the warn-log is dropped).
+
+**Authority note.** `command_for_shortcut` + the W08.3 parity guard are unchanged (the guard tests the binding, not enablement). Menu CLICKS still flow through the host→shell `MenuCommandHandoff` FIFO → `route_menu_command`; #314 unified the KEYBOARD's enablement onto the registry path, NOT the click transport.
+
+**Still open — explicitly NOT closed here:**
+- Play/View accelerator DISPLAY + execution (Play's real keys are the plain `Space`/`Escape` PIE binds, not menu accelerators; View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding).
+- the `AcceleratorTable` conflict UI / conflict population (computed every resolve, still unsurfaced).
+- dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View).
+- plugin menu entries.
+- host→shell FIFO menu-click replacement (clicks still use `MenuCommandHandoff`).
+- generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution beyond the canonical entries.
+- the VISIBILITY predicates (the filtering `predicate`) remain available but UNUSED by `default_editor_menu` — all entries stay visible; only enablement varies.
+
+**Historical preservation.** The W08-accelerator (#308–#311), #304/#305, and PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC (#302) subsections below + all earlier dated entries are preserved byte-identical; their "registry-driven dynamic predicates / per-frame re-resolve … deferred" + bespoke-greying lines are narrowed forward by this subsection (now shipped), not rewritten in place.
+
+**Scope:** docs only (`Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + `plans/BASELINE.md` + `change.md`); no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo change (the menu / enablement rustdoc was made current inside #313 + #314 themselves).
+

 **Forward-only follow-up (W08-ACCELERATOR-DOC-RECONCILE).** Narrows the #304/#305 subsection below ("Still open — accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population"; "Authority: the shown values mirror the live, executing `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` … the deferred W08 EXECUTION work unifies the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`"): the File/Edit accelerator EXECUTION half is now SHIPPED, and the canonical menu is the SOURCE of that routing rather than a mirror of it. Records the four merged PRs that close the W08 accelerator-execution thread.
